question: A sample of 49 sudden infant death syndrome (SIDS) cases had a mean birth weight of 2998 grams. Based on other births in the county, we will assume $\sigma=800$ grams. Calculate the $95 \%$ confidence interval for the mean birth weight of SIDS cases in the county. Interpret your results.
answer:
The $95 \%$ confidence interval for $\mu=2998 \pm(1.96)  rac{800}{\sqrt{49}}=2998 \pm 224=(2774$ to 3222).

Interpret your results. Based on this sample, we have $95 \%$ confidence the population mean $\mu$ is between 2764 and 3212.

meta info.caption: SIDS.
meta info.section: 10.2 Confidence Intervals for $\mu$ When $\sigma$ Is Known
